name: Question Bank — LLM Extraction Quality
description: Validates that Claude correctly extracts structured questions from Cambridge O-Level Computer Science past paper paragraphs. This is the real eval suite that runs against the Presidency Question Bank pipeline.

tests.input: 1. State two reasons why a computer uses binary rather than denary for calculations. [2]

expected.question: State two reasons why a computer uses binary rather than denary for calculations.

tests.input: 2. Study the following Python code: x = 5; y = x + 3; print(y). State the output of this program. [1]

expected.question: Study the following Python code: x = 5; y = x + 3; print(y). State the output of this program.

tests.sourceMaterial: Study the following Python code: x = 5; y = x + 3; print(y). State the output of this program. [1]

tests.input: 3. (a) Define the term 'algorithm'. [1] (b) A programmer needs to sort a list of 1000 numbers. Explain why a bubble sort would be inefficient for this task. [3]

expected.question: Define the term 'algorithm'. A programmer needs to sort a list of 1000 numbers. Explain why a bubble sort would be inefficient for this task.

tests.description: Verify the model doesn't invent mark schemes
tests.input: 4. Convert the denary number 42 to binary. Show your working. [2]

expected.question: Convert the denary number 42 to binary. Show your working.

tests.sourceMaterial: Convert the denary number 42 to binary. Show your working. [2]
